epic.title: Reduce EU checkout & fulfillment friction
epic.problem_statement: EU customers hit a higher-than-acceptable false-decline rate on checkout, and ~0.4% of retries produce duplicate orders because the idempotency window expires before the retry settles. EU delivery ETAs are off by 2-3 days because warehouse routing uses Haversine distance, not road-network distance, and the weighted score doesn't reflect intra-day capacity.
epic.proposed_solution: Introduce a region-aware checkout policy: per-region fraud thresholds, a 72h idempotency window, and a manual-review queue for low-confidence blocks. Replace Haversine distance with a road-network lookup for the EU region, and add intra-day warehouse capacity to the routing score. Add audit log coverage on every 3DS redirect outcome.

user_stories.capability: complete a checkout even when my bank triggers a soft decline
user_stories.benefit: I don't lose my cart and don't get a duplicate charge

user_stories.capability: see and resolve blocked payments before they become chargebacks
user_stories.benefit: my false-decline rate drops and my revenue recovers

user_stories.capability: extend the idempotency window for a specific merchant
user_stories.benefit: retry-heavy tenants don't generate duplicate orders

user_stories.capability: see an accurate delivery ETA at checkout
user_stories.benefit: I can plan to be home and don't miss the drop-off

user_stories.capability: see intra-day capacity reflected in the routing score
user_stories.benefit: I don't get over-allocated when a wave is incoming

acceptance_criteria.given: a customer retries a checkout after a transient gateway timeout
acceptance_criteria.then: the second attempt is rejected as a duplicate and no second order is created

acceptance_criteria.given: an EU debit card is flagged by the fraud rules
acceptance_criteria.when: the rules engine returns a low-confidence block
acceptance_criteria.then: the order is routed to a manual-review queue and the customer sees an estimated resolution time

acceptance_criteria.given: a customer checks out with a EU shipping address
acceptance_criteria.when: the warehouse router computes delivery ETA
acceptance_criteria.then: the ETA is within +/- 1 day of the carrier's published transit time

dependencies.name: github-mcp
dependencies.note: used by the Ideation Agent to harvest PRs and issues

dependencies.name: jira-mcp
dependencies.note: used by the Ideation Agent to harvest issues

epic.effort_rationale: 24 story points across 5 stories. Touches checkout-api and fulfillment-svc; requires a fraud-rules config and a 3DS webhook. The 72h idempotency change is a small data-migration.
epic.risk_summary: Combined checkout + fulfillment + compliance changes touch revenue-critical flows. Mitigate with canary, shadow traffic, and a manual review queue.

tech_debt.finding: Idempotency TTL is hardcoded to 24h. Make this configurable.
Cognitive complexity of weighted_score() is 18; refactor.

architecture_impact.notes: Primary blast radius: checkout-api and fulfillment-svc. No schema migrations to user-facing tables.

fields.summary: Implement Stage 1 Ideation Agent (first concrete sub-agent)
fields.description: Build the first end-to-end sub-agent of the SDLC pipeline.

fields.summary: Idempotency window too short for retry-heavy customers
fields.description: 0.4% of checkout retries land after the 24h idempotency window. Need a longer TTL or two-phase commit.

fields.summary: Fraud rules over-block EU debit cards
fields.description: False-positive rate 3.1% on EU debit cards. Need region tuning.

fields.summary: Road-network distance for EU warehouse routing
fields.description: Replace Haversine with road-network distance in EU region.

repos.name: checkout-api
repos.description: Checkout service: cart, payment, fraud, orders

prs.title: checkout: pre-auth hold on card capture
prs.body: Adds a 5-second pre-auth hold before capturing the card. Reduces chargeback rate on retry-heavy customers.

prs.title: fulfillment: weighted warehouse routing
prs.body: Routes orders to warehouses using a weighted score (capacity, distance, SLA tier). Replaces the round-robin stub.

issues.title: Idempotency keys expire before retries settle
issues.body: We see ~0.4% of checkout retries land after the 24h idempotency window, producing duplicate orders. Need a longer TTL or a two-phase commit.

issues.title: Fraud rules block ~3% of legitimate EU cards
issues.body: False-positive rate on EU debit cards is 3.1%. Need a region-tuned rule set or a manual review path for low-confidence blocks.

issues.title: Warehouse routing: distance calc uses Haversine
issues.body: Replace Haversine with a road-network distance for the EU region to improve ETA accuracy.

items.subject: Card declined but order shows pending
items.description: Customer reports the card was charged but the order is stuck in 'pending'. Need clearer state and an auto-recovery path.

items.subject: EU delivery ETA is off by 2-3 days
items.description: Warehouse routing for EU uses Haversine distance; real road distance gives 2-3 day ETAs.

items.title: Checkout service architecture overview
items.excerpt: Checkout is a Python service behind ALB. The payment gateway is abstracted behind a `PaymentProvider` interface. The idempotency store is Redis with a 24h TTL.

items.title: Warehouse routing: design and constraints
items.excerpt: Routers compute a weighted score from capacity, distance, and SLA tier. Distance is Haversine; we plan to move to road-network distance for the EU region.

items.message: Idempotency TTL is hardcoded to 24h. Make this configurable.
Cognitive complexity of weighted_score() is 18; refactor.

items.headline: EU PSD2 SCA friction cited as #1 checkout abandonment driver
items.implication: Reducing false declines and 3DS redirects has direct revenue impact for EU.

items.headline: Same-day delivery now table-stakes in 3 EU metros
items.implication: Warehouse routing must include road-network distance and intra-day capacity.
